title: Second Conditional: If I had money...
description: If I had money, I would travel. Para situaciones imaginarias o improbables. Error clásico: 'If I would have...'

items.back: If + past simple, would + base verb
items.explanation: El segundo condicional se usa para situaciones IMAGINARIAS o IMPROBABLES en el presente/futuro. La cláusula 'if' usa past simple (aunque el significado es presente/futuro). La cláusula principal usa 'would + verb base'.

cardRef.front: Second Conditional (If + past simple, would + base verb)

items.front: If I had money, I would travel the world.
items.back: Si tuviera dinero, viajaría por el mundo.
items.explanation: Situación irreal: no tengo dinero (ahora). 'Had' = past simple de have, pero el significado es presente/futuro imaginario. 'Would travel' = viajaría. NUNCA: 'If I WOULD HAVE money' — error muy común.

items.front: If I were you, I would talk to her.
items.back: Si yo fuera vos, hablaría con ella.
items.explanation: 'If I were you' es una frase hecha para dar consejos. Con 'to be' se puede usar 'were' para todas las personas (formal): 'If she were taller...', 'If I were a bird...'. 'Was' también es aceptable en inglés informal.

items.front: What would you do if you won the lottery?
items.explanation: El orden puede invertirse: 'What would you do if...?' (sin coma). La cláusula 'if' puede ir al principio o al final. Cuando va al principio, lleva coma; cuando va al final, no lleva coma.

steps.title: Situaciones imaginarias: el segundo condicional
steps.instruction: ¿Cómo decís algo que NO es real ahora pero te lo imaginás? 'Si tuviera un millón de dólares, compraría una casa'. En inglés: 'If I HAD a million dollars, I WOULD buy a house.' Fijate: 'if + pasado' (aunque es presente/futuro), 'would + verbo'. NUNCA 'if I WOULD have'.

steps.title: ¿Cuál es la traducción?
steps.instruction: Elegí la traducción correcta de cada oración condicional.

steps.title: Formá el segundo condicional
steps.instruction: Elegí la forma verbal correcta para cada espacio.

exercises.template: If I {0} the president, I {1} free education for everyone.
She {0} move abroad if she {1} a good job offer.

steps.title: El error clásico: 'would' en la cláusula 'if'
steps.instruction: Detectá el error más común con el segundo condicional.

exercises.sentence: If I would have more time, I would learn guitar.
exercises.correction: If I had more time, I would learn guitar.
exercises.explanation: REGLA DE ORO: NUNCA uses 'would' en la cláusula 'if' del segundo condicional. La cláusula 'if' usa PAST SIMPLE: 'If I HAD...', 'If she WERE...', 'If they KNEW...'. Solo la cláusula principal usa 'would'.

exercises.sentence: If I were you, I wouldn't accept that job.
exercises.explanation: Perfecta. 'If I were you' (consejo), 'I wouldn't accept' (consejo negativo). 'Wouldn't' = would not.

exercises.sentence: I would buy that car if I would have enough money.
exercises.correction: I would buy that car if I had enough money.
exercises.explanation: Mismo error: 'would' no va en la cláusula 'if'. → 'if I HAD enough money'.

exercises.sentence: What would you do if you found a wallet in the street?
exercises.explanation: Correcta. 'Would' en la pregunta principal, 'found' (past simple) en la cláusula 'if'.

steps.title: ¡Lección completa!
steps.instruction: Segundo condicional: If + PAST SIMPLE, WOULD + verb base. Para situaciones imaginarias (ahora no son reales). NUNCA 'if I would...'. 'If I were you...' para consejos. Recordá: first conditional = posible (If it rains, I'll take...). Second conditional = imaginario (If I had wings, I would fly...).
